name: Ctrl+Tab switches tabs on the FIRST press while a rendered preview is focused
comment: End-to-end guard for the 'twice on the rendered page, once in text' bug. Two .md files open as tabs (one window); auto-preview is on, so the front tab's WKWebView is first responder. WebKit swallowed the first Ctrl+Tab, so the tab didn't switch until the second press. This presses ⌃⇥ exactly ONCE and asserts the front tab changed. FAIL-WITHOUT-FIX: with the PreviewWebView interception removed, one press does nothing and the front-tab title stays put, so this goes red. Cross-check: PreviewTabSwitchTests unit-tests the pure decision (tabSwitchIntent); this asserts the user-visible switch the unit test cannot (selectNextTab only moves the selection on-screen). NOTE on initial tab: opening [tab-a, tab-b] leaves the LAST-opened file (tab-b) active; if that assumption is ever wrong the first assert flags it. Requires staged fixtures + the Debug build.
